summary: A small two-story rectangular stone building with a raised cobblestone perimeter foundation and a flat dark-roof inset. Both floors have a continuous row of narrow windows on the visible facades; walls are mostly smooth stone bricks with a cobblestone band between floors and a stone brick slab coping at the roof edge. The roof center is filled with dark nether-brick (or similar dark block) and bordered by light stone slabs. Entrance appears centered on one long side with glowstone above the door (uncertain).

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with raised perimeter foundation
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (windows and banding repeat), roof inset centered

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: wall and decorative band (smooth stone bricks)

elements: continuous narrow windows (two rows: ground and first floor)
single main entrance on one long side with lit block above (uncertain)
raised cobblestone perimeter foundation / short wall
flat inset roof: dark center block surrounded by stone slabs
rebuild_hints: Use a rectangular footprint approx 9 x 13 blocks; center the building on a raised cobblestone perimeter one block high.
Walls: build two full storeys of stonebrick with a one-block-high cobblestone band running horizontally between floors and around corners.
Windows: place narrow 1x2 or 1x1 glass window openings in a continuous row (spacing: roughly windows every 2-3 blocks) on the long facades for both floors.
Roof: create a recessed roof area filled with dark nether-brick (or dark block) and border it with stone slabs one block wide around the top edge.
Entrance: center a doorway on one long side with a glowstone (or light block) directly above the door; include a short cobblestone step from the perimeter wall into the doorway.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from perspective; actual block counts may differ by a few blocks.
Roof center material identified as nether_brick based on color; could be another dark block (concrete, terracotta) instead.
Entrance details: door type and presence of glowstone above are partially occluded; lamp may be glowstone but could be another light-emitting block.
Window exact height (1 or 2 blocks) and internal floor heights are not visible; assumed standard single-block windows stacked across two floors.
Ground trench around site and exact cobblestone perimeter height are visible but exact depth of surrounding dirt area is approximate.
